name: Dozier Bank
description_short: Dozier Bank is a traditional community bank operating since 1910, offering checking, savings, loans, and credit cards with online and mobile banking access.
description_long: Dozier Bank has been serving customers as a community bank since 1910, positioning itself as a 'Safe and Friendly' institution with a long operational history. The bank operates physical locations and provides comprehensive retail banking services through its website at dozierbank.com, with a focus on personal and business customers seeking traditional banking relationships. The bank offers a diverse product line including multiple checking account types (regular checking, NOW accounts, and Money Market Deposit Accounts), savings products (regular savings, certificates of deposit, and Individual Retirement Accounts), and an extensive loan portfolio covering residential real estate, home equity, auto financing, agricultural loans, and business loans. They also issue credit cards with Consumer and Business Platinum applications available for download. Internet banking and a mobile banking app (announced as 'coming soon') provide digital access to accounts. Dozier Bank distinguishes itself through its long operational history, senior citizen special programs, flexible Certificate of Deposit terms (willingness to create custom CD terms beyond standard offerings), and tiered Jumbo MMDA accounts for high-net-worth customers with deposits up to $1 million. The bank also provides financial calculators for auto loans, mortgages, and retirement planning on its website. As a traditional regional bank, Dozier Bank serves customers who prefer established institutions with physical branch locations and personal banking relationships. However, the bank's website content is limited, with an incomplete 'About Us' page and minimal detail on rates, fees beyond minimum balance requirements, or specific loan terms. The announced 'coming soon' mobile banking app (as of the 2023 copyright date) suggests delayed digital modernization compared to larger competitors.
services: NOW accounts (interest-earning checking for personal accounts)
Money Market Deposit Accounts (MMDA) with tiered options including Jumbo MMDAs
Regular savings accounts with quarterly interest compounding
Certificates of Deposit with flexible custom terms
Credit cards (Consumer Platinum and Business Platinum)
Home equity and home improvement loans
Mobile banking app (announced as coming soon)
Financial calculators (auto loans, mortgage, retirement planning)
pros: Operating since 1910 with established brand and long customer history
No service charge on checking accounts if minimum monthly balance maintained ($500 personal, $1,500 business)
Special senior citizen programs available with contact to bank
Flexible Certificate of Deposit terms—bank will create custom CD timeframes beyond standard 3-month to 3-year options
MMDA accounts offer higher interest rates than standard checking with only 3 check/transfer limits per cycle
Tiered Jumbo MMDA accounts for high-net-worth depositors up to $1 million minimum
Comprehensive loan products including residential, commercial, agricultural, auto, and personal lending
cons: Website incomplete—'About Us' page cuts off mid-content with no bank history, leadership, or community information provided
Mobile banking app announced as 'coming soon' with no launch date given (website dated 2023)
No current interest rates displayed on website—customers must call 334-496-3592 for rate quotes
Service charges apply if minimum balances drop ($22 fee for MMDA if balance falls below $2,500)
Limited online presence and digital modernization compared to larger regional and national banks
best_for: Customers seeking a traditional, established community bank with physical branch locations
High-net-worth individuals interested in Jumbo MMDA accounts with substantial minimum deposits
Small business owners preferring relationship banking with local loan officers
Senior citizens eligible for special banking programs
diagnosis: Dozier Bank is best for customers in its geographic service area who value long-established community banking institutions and prefer in-person relationships with local loan officers. The main caveats are the incomplete digital presence (incomplete website pages, delayed mobile app launch), lack of published interest rates requiring customer contact for quotes, and minimum balance requirements that may not suit customers seeking completely free checking accounts.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
